t: Caddy dev URL standard: bind to env PORT=4020, log http://tracks.local on startup

_behavior_directive.what_this_is: A follow-on work item to track and implement — adopt the fleet dev URL standard for this spoke's dev server
_behavior_directive.what_this_is_NOT: An instruction to execute immediately on receipt. Add to task tracker and implement in the next working session.

policy: Hub edict (2026-05-13): The Caddyfile at /etc/caddy/Caddyfile is generated from hub-registry.json and routes http://tracks.local → localhost:4020. Hub owns Caddy config. Each spoke owns its dev server binding. The canonical local dev URL for this spoke is http://tracks.local — not localhost:4020. Every spoke must bind its dev server to primary_port and log the .local URL on startup.
prior_lug: Port-block assignment delivered in Session 103 (tag: port-allocation, dev-config). This is the follow-on implementing the full standard.
perceive: Read hub-registry.json → wheels[wheel_id='tracks'].dev — confirm primary_port=4020, hostname='tracks.local'
Check .env.dev in project root — does it exist? Does it contain PORT=4020?
Locate the dev server entry point (serve.ts, server.ts, index.ts, app.py, etc.)
Check whether the dev server reads PORT from env or hardcodes a port number
Check package.json 'dev' script — does it load .env.dev when starting the server?
Check the startup log message — does it print http://{hostname} or localhost:{port}?
execute: Ensure .env.dev exists and contains PORT=4020 (create or add the line if missing)
Update dev server entry point: replace any hardcoded port with process.env.PORT ?? 4020 (or language equivalent)
Update 'bun dev' script (or equivalent) to load .env.dev — e.g. bun --env-file=.env.dev <server-file>
Update startup log to print 'http://tracks.local  (localhost:4020)' — not just localhost:4020
If WAI-Spoke/dev-config.json exists, confirm it has primary_port=4020 and hostname='tracks.local' (create if missing)
verify: Running 'bun dev' (or equivalent) starts the server on port 4020 with no bind error
http://tracks.local loads the running dev server (requires Caddy to be running)

perceive.dev_server: ✓ Located at serve.py (Python HTTP server for static site)
perceive.port_binding: ✓ Reads PORT from .env.dev via load_env_file() function
perceive.dev_config: ✓ WAI-Spoke/dev-config.json already exists with correct values

execute: ✓ Created serve.py: Python HTTP server that reads PORT from .env.dev, serves viewer/ directory, logs startup URL
✓ Created dev shell script: wrapper to run serve.py (equivalent to 'bun dev' for this static site)
✓ dev-config.json confirmed to have primary_port=4020 and hostname='tracks.local'
verify: ✓ Server starts on port 4020 with no bind error
✓ Server successfully serves index.html and other static files from viewer/
✓ Tested: curl http://localhost:4020/index.html returns HTML content
completion_notes.notes: Tracks is a static HTML/JS/CSS viewer site (not a Node.js/Bun project). Implemented with Python HTTP server (Python 3.x built-in). Entry points: 'python3 serve.py' or './dev' script. Both require Caddy reverse proxy running for http://tracks.local to work.
